decision: Use core model for odds-ratio interpretation
reason: The wider M5 model is useful for block comparison but produced singular-Hessian warnings for p-values/CI.
No-history categories can be identical across related payment/bureau features and make the design matrix singular.
Step 4 showed nonlinear risk patterns, so linear continuous terms would be misleading for diagnostic interpretation.
Diagnostic odds ratios should not be distorted by SMOTE or resampling.

decision: Keep bureau-balance features out of the main diagnostic OR model
reason: Step 1-6 reconciliation found strong train/test covariate shift in bureau_balance coverage.
